title: analysis_modes.tsv row — what an analysis needs and how to default the rest
description: One row per (analysis_type × mode). Tells the resolver: 'for analysis A in mode M, the user must supply these required_dimensions; everything else is filled by the named policies'. The policies are short tags that resolve.py knows how to match against the six registries (e.g. site_policy='thin500_per_chr' looks for a site_set with parent='thin500_global' and interval_set covering the requested chromosome).

required_dimensions.description: Comma-separated list of dimensions the USER must supply. e.g. 'sample_set,chromosome'. The resolver fills the rest from policies.

interval_policy.description: Tag the resolver matches against interval_sets. Recognized: genome_all, chromosome_full, candidate_interval, same_as_input.

site_policy.description: Tag matched against site_sets. Recognized: thin500_global, thin500_per_chr, candidate_sites, karyotype_calls, same_as_input.

group_policy.description: Tag matched against group_sets. Recognized: family_population, family_karyotype, optional_groups, same_as_input.

value_policy.description: Tag matched against input_values. Default: BEAGLE matching the resolved (sample_set, site_set, interval_set). Recognized: beagle_matching, none.

produces.description: Output kind tag (e.g. 'relatedness_res', 'pedigree_result', 'mendelian_result'). Used to chain modes (ngspedigree.required_dimensions includes 'ngsrelate_result' which means 'a previous analysis_results row whose produces == relatedness_res').

module_name.description: FK into module_registry.tsv — the biomod module that implements this analysis. The page combines DATA readiness (RESULT_READY / RUN_READY / …) with MODULE readiness (installed / ready / stale) when displaying a step. Empty allowed for purely conceptual modes.
